rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL WALTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT DAP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IDENTIFYING DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT AND THE GENERAL ORDER INTERROGATORY ANSWERS IS AN INEFFECTIVE BASIS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF UNDER SCHEIDING. RELIANCE ON THE RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FILED WITH THE REPLY WOULD BE CONTRARY TO C.C.P. SECTION 437C. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCISCO MANZO VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Case Title: PAUL FRANCIS PELOSI VS. GARY ROSSI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And Ot Enforce The Settlement Agreement;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 6. CROSS-DEFENDANT GARY ROSSI'S Motion To Recall And Quash Writ Of Execution And TO Enforce The Settlement Agreement IS DENIED FOR LACK OF JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED ALL PARTIES WITH PREJUDICE ON DECEMBER 14, 2007 AND NO REQUEST WAS MADE TO THE COURT TO RETAIN JURISDICTION DURING THE PENDENCY OF THE ACTION. WACKEEN V MALIS (2002) 97 CAL. APP. 4TH 429, 440. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED FARMERS AGENTS ASSOCIATION INC ON BEHALF VS. FARMERS GROUP INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Farmers Group, Inc'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 8. DEFEMDANTS FARMER GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S ALTER EGO THEORY MAY NOT BE ADDRESSED ON DEMURRER. THE REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED FARMERS AGENTS ASSOCIATION INC ON BEHALF VS. FARMERS GROUP INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FARMERS NEW WORLD LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE, FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE AND MID-CENTURY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. ASSOCIATIONAL STANDING HAS BEEN ALLEGED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE SECOND THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR THESE VERY CLAIMS WERE RAISED IN THE ALAMEDA ACTION. PLAINTIFF WILL BE GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND ONLY IF PLAINTIFF CAN ALLEGE THAT THESE CLAIMS FIRST AROSE WITHIN FOUR YEARS OF THE DATE OF THE FILING OF THIS COMPLAINT. THE REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ARLENE CAPIZZI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR, ENTIRE ACTION DISMISSED ON JANUARY 25, 2008. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DAN MESCHER VS. PACIFIC,GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 20, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT THE OPPOSING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE ANOTHER SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MOHSEN SULTAN VS. CONSERVATION AND LIQUIDATION OFFICE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JANUARY 31, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANTS CONSERVATION AND LIQUIDATION OFFICE AND SCOTT PEARCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES A F.E.H.A. CLAIM AND THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES A TAMENY CLAIM. THE DECISION IN WALRATH V SPRINKEL (2002) 99 CAL APP 4TH 1237 HOLDS THAT A SUPERVISOR MAY BE LIABLE FOR RETALIATION. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, F.E.H.A. IS A REMEDIAL STATUTE AND DOES NOT DISPLACE COMMON LAW TORTS. THE FACTS ALLEGED PLEAD OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. ALTHOUGH THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION HAVE SIMILAR ALLEGATIONS, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE FROM THE PLEADINGS WHETHER THE CLAIMS ARE DUPLICATIVE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition Of Relief Against Forfeiture Of A Lease
